message:
style: cargo fmt across fnx crates (no semantic changes)

Pure `cargo fmt --all` output, applied without any logic edits, to
keep `cargo fmt --check` green for the workspace. No behavior change,
no API change, no test change — only line wrapping and brace
placement normalization in four spots that had drifted from rustfmt
defaults:

* `crates/fnx-conformance/src/lib.rs`:
  rewraps the multi-arg `fnx_algorithms::modularity(&context.graph,
  &communities, 1.0, &weight_attr)` call inside the modularity
  fixture handler so the indented continuation lines up with rustfmt
  style. Same expression, same `.expect(...)` message, same
  `context.modularity_result = Some(result);` follow-up.

* `crates/fnx-generators/src/lib.rs`:
  collapses a hand-wrapped `let _ = graph.add_edge(node_labels[*u]
  .clone(), node_labels[*v].clone());` back onto one rustfmt line in
  the `random_regular_graph` block.

* `crates/fnx-python/src/lib.rs` (PyMultiGraph::copy edge loop):
  - Rewraps the `(u, v, key) = (snapshot.left.clone(),
    snapshot.right.clone(), snapshot.key)` destructuring back to one
    line per rustfmt.
  - Rewraps the `new_graph.inner.add_edge_with_key_and_attrs(u.clone(),
    v.clone(), key, rust_attrs)` call to the rustfmt-preferred
    receiver-on-its-own-line form. Edge insertion order
    (br-copyedgeord) and attribute handling are unchanged.

* `crates/fnx-readwrite/src/lib.rs` (GraphML default-text branch):
  promotes the previously inline `match decode_graphml_text_content(e) { ... }`
  arm to a block-bodied arm so rustfmt can wrap the long
  `self.record("read_graphml", DecisionAction::FailClosed, &warning,
  1.0)` and `DecisionAction::FullValidate` calls properly. All match
  arm bodies, including the strict-mode `FailClosed` early-return,
  the `warnings.push(warning.clone())`, and the
  `current_key_default_text.clear(); current_key_default_key = None;`
  reset, are byte-identical to the previous version — the only
  change is how the text wraps in the source.

Verified byte-identical AST on each touched function: structural
formatting only.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
